title: Smoky Roasted American Morcilla Biryani
description: A tangy, fluffy American take on biryani that pairs smoky roasted morcilla with fragrant basmati rice and a homemade spice blend, perfect for a diabetic-friendly dinner.

ingredients.name: spice blend
ingredients.notes: cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, cardamom, smoked paprika

instructions: Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear; soak for 20 minutes.
In a large oven-safe pot, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add diced onion and sauté until golden.
Add the minced garlic and ginger paste; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the diced tomatoes and cook until they soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the sliced morcilla, the spice blend, and salt; sauté everything together for 5 minutes so the morcilla browns slightly.
Whisk in the plain yogurt and green chili, letting the mixture simmer for 2 minutes to blend the flavors.
Drain the soaked rice and add it to the pot, gently folding it into the sauce. Pour in 4 cups of water, bring to a boil, then cover tightly.
Transfer the pot to a preheated oven at 180°C (350°F) and bake for 25 minutes, until the rice is cooked and fluffy.
Remove from the oven, fluff the rice with a fork, and stir in the chopped cilantro.
Serve hot, garnished with extra cilantro if desired, and enjoy the smoky, tangy flavors of this American morcilla biryani.
